signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at 18.5% extreme) vs RBNZ hawkish shift. New Zealand inflation expectations jumped sharply as the market prices a hike bias for the 27 May meeting. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% — 150bp spread currently favors USD, but crowded spec shorts face significant unwind risk if RBNZ confirms the hawkish outlook. NZD/USD at 0.5944.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.150 — AUD 1W avg +0.560% vs JPY 1W avg -0.587% creates a 1.147% G8 spread. RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% — +360bp carry premium remains the primary driver as JPY remains subdued despite OECD calls for BoJ rate normalization to 2% by 2027. Retail 87% short AUD/JPY diverges from COT LF net long +58,994, suggesting retail capitulation risk if the carry trade extends.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Correlation Break + Monetary Policy
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.698 vs US 10Y: corr=+0.526 vs 252d norm=+0.089 (z=-1.96σ). The pair is trading with an abnormally high sensitivity to US yields as hot US CPI (3.8% y/y) lifts Fed hike bets. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% — 300bp spread keeps the pair bid despite hawkish BoJ rhetoric. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 confirms institutional carry persistence.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Cross-Asset Correlation
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1737 vs EuroStoxx: corr=+0.689 vs 252d norm=-0.121 (z=+1.79σ). EUR is currently tracking equity sentiment rather than its traditional inverse relationship. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% — 175bp spread favors USD, while EUR COT LF net short -21,011 indicates specs are positioned for further downside as US inflation picks up.
